content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

October 6, 1989


Doris Christen
City Clerk
330 West Twentieth Avenue
San Mateo, CA 94403

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-89-542

Dear Ms. Christen:

You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided to you concerning the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act.¹

Your letter, dated September 18, 1989, correctly states our advice that because the general municipal election for the City of San Mateo was cancelled, the Act's provisions relating to pre-election campaign statements would not apply. (Government Code Section 84200.5.) A copy of your letter is enclosed for your convenience.

Please contact me at (916) 322-5662 if you have additional questions.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

By: Lynda Cassady
Political Reform-Consultant

Enclosure


¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916) 322-5660

FPPC

SEP 19 10 45 AM '89

OFFICE OF THE CITY CLERK

330 WEST TWENTIETH AVENUE
SAN MATEO, CALIFORNIA 94403

TELEPHONE: (415) 377-3420

September 18, 1989


Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Dear Sirs:

This is to inform you that the General Municipal Election scheduled
to be held in the City of San Mateo on November 7, 1989 to elect
three City Council Members was cancelled by the San Mateo City
Council at their meeting of September 6, 1989. The election was
cancelled in accordance with Elections Code Section 22843.5 and
the three persons nominated were appointed to fill the three
offices.

Since there will be no election, it is my understanding after calling
your agency that pre-election campaign statements will not be required
to be filed. If this is not correct, please notify me right away
so that September 28 filing deadlines can be met. Since all three
nominees were incumbents, they will be filing semi-annual statements
covering this six-months period in January 1990.

Sincerely,

[signature]

DORIS CHRISTEN
CITY CLERK

89-542

FPPC
SEP 19 10 45 AM '89

OFFICE OF THE CITY CLERK

330 WEST TWENTIETH AVENUE
SAN MATEO, CALIFORNIA 94403

TELEPHONE: (415) 377-3420

September 18, 1989


Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA  95804-0807

Dear Sirs:

     This is to inform you that the General Municipal Election scheduled
to be held in the City of San Mateo on November 7, 1989 to elect
three City Council Members was cancelled by the San Mateo City
Council at their meeting of September 6, 1989.  The election was
cancelled in accordance with Elections Code Section 22843.5 and
the three persons nominated were appointed to fill the three
offices.

     Since there will be no election, it is my understanding after calling
your agency that pre-election campaign statements will not be required
to be filed.  If this is not correct, please notify me right away
so that September 28 filing deadlines can be met.  Since all three
nominees were incumbents, they will be filing semi-annual statements
covering this six-months period in January 1990.

Sincerely,

[signature]
DORIS CHRISTEN
CITY CLERK

September 25, 1989


Doris Christen
City Clerk
330 West Twentieth Avenue
San Mateo, CA  94403

Re:  Letter No. 89-542

Dear Ms. Christen:

We received your letter requesting confirmation of advice under the Political Reform Act on September 20, 1989.  Your letter has been assigned to our Technical Assistance and Analysis Division for response.  If you have any questions, you may contact that division directly at (916) 322-5662.

If the letter is appropriate for confirmation without further analysis, we will attempt to expedite our response.  A confirming response will be released after it has gone through our approval process.  If the letter is not appropriate for this treatment, the staff person assigned to prepare the response will contact you shortly to advise you.  In such cases, the normal analysis, review and approval process will be followed.

You should be aware that your letter and our response are public records which may be disclosed to any interested person upon receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Sincerely,

[signature]

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

KED:plh:confadvl


428 J Street, Suite 800  •  P.O. Box 807  •  Sacramento CA 95804-0807  •  (916) 322-5660
